props.text: Comprehensive overview of infrastructure setup, deployment architecture, monitoring, operational procedures, and disaster recovery planning.

props.body:
Vercel - Next.js frontend hosting (edge caching, automatic deployments)
• Railway/Heroku - Strapi backend hosting (PostgreSQL database included)
• Cloudflare - DNS, DDoS protection, CDN
• AWS S3 - Media storage for images and files
• SendGrid - Email delivery service

Next.js 15 - Server Components, App Router
• Strapi CMS - Headless CMS with REST/GraphQL APIs
• PostgreSQL - Primary database
• Redis - Caching layer (if needed for queues)
• TanStack Query - Client-side caching

npm run dev - Start Next.js development server
• npm run strapi-dev - Start Strapi development server
• Database: SQLite or local PostgreSQL
• Environment: .env.local for local overrides

Automatic from pull requests on Vercel
• Database: Staging PostgreSQL instance
• Features: Full feature set with preview data

Vercel deployment from main branch
• Database: Production PostgreSQL
• CDN edge locations globally (Vercel)
• High availability and auto-scaling

API Configuration - REST/GraphQL endpoints, webhooks
• CMS Operations - Content management, user roles, backups
• Database Management - Migrations, backups, recovery
• Deployment Pipelines - CI/CD, previews, production
• Monitoring & Alerting - Error tracking, performance metrics
• Security - Access control, secrets management, compliance
• Scaling - Auto-scaling configuration, load testing

Uptime target: 99.9% (43 minutes downtime/month allowed)
• Response time: < 200ms median (P99 < 1s)
• Database: 99.99% uptime SLA
• Deployment frequency: Multiple deployments per day
• Mean time to recovery (MTTR): < 15 minutes for critical issues
• Monitoring: Real-time alerting for anomalies
